methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $53M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Biofire is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Kai Kloepfer that has raised $52.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $52.9M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $106M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M) establishes a median of $300M and a range spanning $150M to $500M.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $90M to $345M, with a median estimate of $203M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty in peer selection and stage-appropriate step-up assumptions. The peer-median method carries particular weight given the specificity of same-stage and same-sector filtering, though limited sample size (n=3) introduces selection risk. Stage-progression methodology is more conservative but relies on using total raised as a valuation proxy, which may understate earlier-stage economics. Key uncertainties include whether Biofire's specific business model and capital efficiency justify positioning toward the higher or lower end of the peer range.
